title: Why Higher Wages Beat Tax Cuts
subtitle: 经济增长新叙事：涨工资比减税更管用

paragraphs.en: One of the great successes of the Republican Party in recent decades is the relentless propagation of a simple formula for economic growth: tax cuts. The formula doesn't work, but that has not affected its popularity. And while the cult of tax cuts has attracted many critics, it lacks for obvious rivals. Democratic politicians have tended to campaign on helping people left behind by economic growth. When Democrats do talk about encouraging economic growth, they often sound like Republicans.
paragraphs.zh: 共和党近几十年来的一大成功，就是不遗余力地传播一个简单的经济增长公式：减税。这个公式并不奏效，但这并未影响它的受欢迎程度。尽管对减税的狂热崇拜招致众多批评，它却缺少明显的替代方案。民主党政治家在竞选中倾向于关注那些被经济增长抛在后面的人。而当民主党人真的谈论如何促进经济增长时，他们听起来又往往像共和党人。

paragraphs.en: This is not just a political problem for Democrats; it is an economic problem for the United States. The nation needs a better story about the drivers of economic growth. The painful lessons of recent decades point to a promising candidate: higher wages. Raising the wages of American workers ought to be the priority of economic policymakers. We'd all be better off paying less attention to quarterly updates on the growth of the nation's gross domestic product and focusing instead on the growth of workers' paychecks.
paragraphs.zh: 这不仅是民主党面临的政治问题，也是美国面临的经济问题。这个国家需要一个关于经济增长驱动力的更佳叙事。近几十年来的惨痛教训指向了一个有希望的方向：更高的工资。提高美国工人的工资理应成为经济政策制定者的首要任务。我们最好少关注国内生产总值的季度增长数据，而多关注工人薪水的增长。

paragraphs.en: Set aside, for the moment, the familiar argument for higher wages: fairness. The argument here is that higher wages can fuel the engine of economic growth. Perhaps the most famous illustration of the benefits is the story of Henry Ford's decision in 1914 to pay $5 a day to workers on his Model T assembly lines. He did it to increase production — he was paying a premium to maintain a reliable workforce. The unexpected benefit was that Ford's factory workers became Ford customers, too. The same logic still holds: Consumption drives the American economy, and workers who are paid more can spend more.
paragraphs.zh: 暂且抛开关于提高工资的那个熟悉论点——公平。这里的论点是，更高的工资能够为经济增长引擎提供燃料。关于其益处最著名的例证，或许是亨利·福特1914年决定向T型车装配线工人支付每天5美元工资的故事。他这样做是为了提高产量——支付高薪以维持一支稳定的劳动力队伍。意想不到的好处是，福特的工厂工人也成了福特的顾客。同样的逻辑至今依然成立：消费驱动着美国经济，工资更高的工人消费能力也更强。

paragraphs.en: Mainstream economists insist that it is impossible to order up a sustainable increase in wages because compensation levels reflect the unerring judgment of market forces. The conventional wisdom held that productivity growth was the only route to higher wages. Through that lens, efforts to negotiate higher wages were counterproductive. Minimum-wage laws would raise unemployment because there was only so much money in the wage pool, and if some people got more, others would get none.
paragraphs.zh: 主流经济学家坚持认为，不可能凭空要求工资持续增长，因为薪酬水平反映的是市场力量准确无误的判断。传统观点认为，生产率增长是提高工资的唯一途径。从这一视角来看，争取更高工资的努力只会适得其反。最低工资法将推高失业率，因为工资池里的钱就那么多，如果有些人拿得更多，其他人就分不到了。

paragraphs.en: It was in the context of this worldview that it became popular to argue that tax cuts would drive prosperity. Rich people would invest, productivity would increase, wages would rise. In the real world, things are more complicated. Wages are influenced by a tug of war between employers and workers, and employers have been winning.
paragraphs.zh: 正是在这种世界观的背景下，减税能驱动繁荣的论点才变得流行起来。富人将进行投资，生产率将提高，工资将随之上涨。然而在现实世界中，事情要复杂得多。工资受到雇主与工人之间拉锯战的影响，而雇主一直是赢家。

paragraphs.en: One clear piece of evidence is the widening gap between productivity growth and wage growth since roughly 1970. Productivity has more than doubled; wages have lagged far behind. A focus on wage growth would provide an antidote to the attractive simplicity of the belief in the magical power of tax cuts.
paragraphs.zh: 一个明显的证据是，自1970年前后以来，生产率增长与工资增长之间的差距不断扩大。生产率已翻了一倍多，而工资却远远落后。将关注点转向工资增长，将为那种迷信减税魔力的简单化信念提供一剂解药。
